new_classes.definition_en: The process of producing piquette by re-fermenting grape marc (grape pomace) with water, as defined by Additional Note 9 to Chapter 22 of the CN.
A quality of a fermented beverage indicating that it was produced from grape marc (grape pomace), distinguishing piquette (CN 2206 00 10) from other fermented beverages under heading 2206.
A fermented beverage produced from grape marc (pomace) re-fermented with water, classified under CN subheading 2206 00 10 as defined by Additional Note 9 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criterion — that piquette is produced from grape marc (pomace) re-fermented with water — is captured via the GrapeMarcOriginQuality and PiquetteProductionProcess. However, the precise legal definition is given in Additional Note 9 to Chapter 22, whose full text is not provided in the legal notes supplied here (only a reference is given). If Additional Note 9 contains further quantitative or compositional criteria (e.g. minimum/maximum alcoholic strength, specific water-to-marc ratios, or exclusion of other production methods), those conditions could not be encoded and the coverage score is reduced accordingly. The sibling classes under OtherFermentedBeverages2206 (other products in that heading) are discriminated by the grape-marc origin quality and the specific production process, which are unique to piquette within heading 2206.
